# UC-17.1.24 · SonicWall Site-to-Site VPN IKE and Tunnel Down Events

## Description

Branch and partner connectivity depends on stable SonicWall VPN tunnels. IKE failures, PFS mismatches, or dead peer detection events often indicate credential rotation errors, routing changes, or active interference—each with different response playbooks.

## Value

Reduces branch downtime by alerting network operations before users open tickets, and surfaces potential credential or configuration attacks on VPN termination points.

## Implementation

Ingest complete VPN subsystem logs; avoid filtering at the forwarder. Tag each appliance with site metadata. Correlate with certificate expiry and shared-secret change tickets. Tune phrases to your SonicOS version message catalog.

## SPL

```spl
index=netfw (sourcetype="dell:sonicwall" OR sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall") earliest=-24h
| search (IKE OR "IPSec" OR "VPN" OR "tunnel") AND (down OR failed OR "phase 1" OR "phase 2" OR timeout OR disconnect)
| stats count by host, name, peer, msg
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Timeline (VPN errors), Table (peer gateway, message), Single value (active tunnels down).
